key_insight: A coupled thermal-mechanical ABAQUS model shows that welding heat creates steep temperature gradients that drive residual stress and deformation in 6082 aluminum butt joints.
engineering_relevance: The results help engineers predict weld-zone performance and guide welding-process optimization and structural design for aluminum alloys.
method: Built a 3D temperature-displacement coupled model in ABAQUS.
Ran transient thermal analysis to compute the welding temperature field.
Mapped the temperature field into mechanical analysis to estimate residual stress and deformation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a clear temperature gradient from weld zone to base material, with residual stress concentrated in the weld seam and heat-affected zone and visible deformation near the weld.
watch_item: The summary is based on numerical simulation only, so model accuracy depends on boundary conditions, material parameters, and validation against experiments.
paper_title: Thermal-Mechanical Analysis of Residual Stress and Deformation in 6082 Aluminum Alloy Welded Joints
